mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and running workloads. It is useful for continuous security monitoring and compliance in Kubernetes environments. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration to avoid false positives and the potential performance overhead during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator in the 'trivy-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator with a specific version.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a TrivyConfiguration resource to define the scanning behavior of the Trivy Operator.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: trivy-config
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  imagePullSecrets:
    - name: my-registry-secret
  severity: HIGH
EOF

```

> This creates a TrivyConfiguration with specified settings.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: trivy-config
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  imagePullSecrets:
    - name: my-registry-secret
  severity: HIGH
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Trivy Operator installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Trivy Operator pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the trivy-system namespace to verify installation.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that any configurations and scan results will be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important configurations. This step upgrades the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivy-config-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backs up the current TrivyConfiguration.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivy-config-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Trivy Operator pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' and ensure the necessary RBAC permissions are set.

troubleshooting.title: TrivyConfiguration not applied
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The TrivyConfiguration resource may have syntax errors or invalid fields.

**Fix:**
Validate the YAML syntax and ensure all required fields are correctly specified.

troubleshooting.title: Scan results not appearing
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may not have permissions to access the required resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account used by the Trivy Operator has the necessary permissions to access the resources being scanned.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Aqua Security Helm repository may not be added or updated correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run 'helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts' and 'helm repo update'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom image scanning configurations and improved performance of vulnerability scans.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace with a 'Running' status when executing 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: trivy-config
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  imagePullSecrets:
    - name: my-registry-secret
  severity: HIGH
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
